methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Teramount is a Series B-stage semiconductor company founded by Hesham Taha and Abraham Israel that has raised $50M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 6 private peers in chips and semiconductors at similar or adjacent stages, plus 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) held as reference points only.

Two primary methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $50M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $95M. Peer-Median anchors to a cohort of 6 same-sector companies including Etched ($5.0B, Series A), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series A), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), Substrate ($1.0B, Series A), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C), producing a median valuation of $1.0B with a range of $500M–$5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (24.4x median across public semiconductors) was not applicable due to absence of Teramount's revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $81M to $1.1B, with a median of $548M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression floor reflects capital deployed, while the peer median reflects heterogeneous growth trajectories and market timing across the private frontier-semiconductor cohort. Revenue visibility and technical differentiation remain key determinants within this range.
